files_to_change.change: Add per-node lock primitive (nodeLocksMu + nodeLocks map[uint64]*sync.Mutex) and exported EdgeLog.LockNode(id) (unlock func()). AppendEdge now holds the node lock for its entire body (getOrOpenWriter through the durable Append call), not just the writer-map lookup. OpenEdgeLog initializes the new map. No change to TruncateNode's own internals or to ReadNodeAfter's signature/behavior.

Compact's node loop acquires log.LockNode(id) immediately before calling ReadNodeAfter, invokes new test-only hook compactNodeLockedHook(id) if set (still holding the lock), and keeps the lock held (deferred release tracked in a slice) across WriteCSR/saveCompactState, releasing it only after that node's TruncateNode call in the final loop (or immediately if maxSeg < 0 - nothing to protect). All early-return error paths release every currently-held node lock before returning. Compact's public signature is unchanged; new unexported var compactNodeLockedHook func(id uint64), nil in production, added for the new test.

Add TestCompactConcurrentAppendNotLost using compactNodeLockedHook to force a concurrent AppendEdge into the read-then-truncate window, asserting the concurrently-appended edge is not lost and is merged by a subsequent Compact call.

out_of_scope_untouched: engine/wal/writer.go (no change needed - existing WriteSegmentFloor/latestSegmentNum semantics already correctly handle the resumed-writer case once TruncateNode's segment listing is protected by the node lock)
risk_notes: AppendEdge calls to a node currently being compacted will now block for the duration of that Compact run's WriteCSR/saveCompactState calls (previously they could proceed immediately, at the cost of the data-loss bug being fixed). This is a bounded, disclosed availability tradeoff, not a correctness regression; AppendEdge to OTHER nodes is unaffected (per-node lock, not global).

Lock acquisition order is always nodeLock(id) before EdgeLog.mu (via getOrOpenWriter/TruncateNode) in both AppendEdge and Compact's paths - no deadlock risk given the existing single-compactor assumption already baked into compact.go's sidecar mechanism.

nodeLocks map entries are never evicted (grows one entry per distinct fileID ever appended, for the process lifetime) - matches this package's existing acceptance of unbounded per-node directory/state growth (see compact.go's edgeLogNodeIDs doc comment); not addressed here as out of scope for a single-commit subtask.
